entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The management of natural resources, particularly in dry regions, to ensure sustainability and support livelihoods.

entities.name: DRY REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas characterized by low rainfall and fragile ecosystems where agriculture is the primary occupation.

entities.name: FRAGILE ECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Sensitive ecological systems in dry regions that are under pressure from natural resource management challenges.

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The primary occupation of people living in dry regions and the main driver of natural resource use.

entities.name: WATERSHED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of managing land and water resources within a watershed, historically focused on soil and water conservation.

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION MEASURES
entities.description: Specific technical interventions used in conventional watershed management to prevent soil erosion and conserve water.

entities.name: INTEGRATED WATERSHED MANAGEMENT MODEL
entities.description: A holistic approach to watershed management that integrates genetic and natural resource management with participatory strategies.

entities.name: FARMER-PARTICIPATORY APPROACH
entities.description: A component of the integrated watershed management model that involves farmers in the decision-making and implementation process.

entities.name: NEW SCIENCE TOOLS
entities.description: Modern scientific instruments and techniques used to enhance the integrated watershed management model.

entities.name: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A strategy (IGNRM) providing site-specific solutions by combining genetic resource management with natural resource management.

entities.name: CONSORTIUM OF INSTITUTIONS
entities.description: A collaborative group of multi-institutional partners providing technical backstopping and knowledge exchange.

entities.name: MONITORING AND EVALUATION
entities.description: The continuous process of assessing progress and outcomes conducted by stakeholders in the watershed model.

entities.name: COMMUNITY AND WOMEN EMPOWERMENT
entities.description: The process of increasing the capacity and influence of community members and women within the watershed management framework.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION
entities.description: The goal of safeguarding the environment through integrated management practices.

entities.name: CONSORTIUM APPROACH
entities.description: A strategic framework involving multi-institutional collaboration to link research and development for sustainable resource management.

entities.name: STRATEGIC AND DEVELOPMENTAL RESEARCH
entities.description: Research activities linked directly to farmers' fields to reduce the lag in technology transfer.

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in land quality and productivity, which is reduced through the consortium strategy.

entities.name: RURAL LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing the basic necessities of rural people, improved through increased incomes via the consortium approach.

relationships.target: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
